\tzpentry{ID3839}{With Symplectic Form Yields Negative Relation}
\paragraph{Canonical Statement.}
ID3839 introduces With Symplectic Form Yields Negative Relation as a source-extracted canonical equation candidate. The equation is promoted from the Tier A equation-source intake and remains pending source-context expansion, duplicate review against the earlier TZPID corpus, Wolfram normalization, symbol-key enrichment, and derivation/proof-lane construction.
\paragraph{Canonical Equation.}
\[
\(X_H\) with the symplectic form yields the negative differential of the Hamiltonian. The general identity \(\dot{F}=\{F,H\}\)
\]
